name: Aadhaar Card
purpose: 12-digit unique identity number issued by UIDAI to every Indian resident. Used as primary identity and address proof for all government services.
issuing_authority: UIDAI – Unique Identification Authority of India
required_documents: Proof of Identity: Passport, Voter ID, PAN Card, or Driving Licence
Proof of Address: Utility bill (not older than 3 months), Bank passbook, or Rental agreement
Date of Birth proof: Birth Certificate or School leaving certificate
steps: Visit nearest Aadhaar Seva Kendra or Common Service Centre (CSC)
Fill the Aadhaar Enrolment Form
Submit documents and provide biometrics (fingerprints, iris scan, photograph)
Receive 14-digit Enrolment ID on acknowledgment slip
Download e-Aadhaar from uidai.gov.in after 2-3 days or receive physical card in 60-90 days
processing_time: 60–90 days for physical card; e-Aadhaar in 2–3 days

name: Ration Card
purpose: Official document for purchasing subsidised food grains (rice, wheat, sugar) from Fair Price Shops under the Public Distribution System (PDS).
issuing_authority: State Food and Civil Supplies Department / District Collector's Office
required_documents: Aadhaar card for all family members
Proof of residence: electricity bill or rental agreement
Passport-size photographs of all family members
Surrender certificate if transferring from another state
steps: Visit State Food and Civil Supplies Department portal or nearest tehsil/CSC
Fill Form-1 (Application for New Ration Card)
Attach all required documents
Submit to Block Development Officer (BDO) or upload online
Collect acknowledgment slip with application number
Card issued within 15–30 working days after verification

name: Caste Certificate
purpose: Official document certifying that a person belongs to a Scheduled Caste (SC), Scheduled Tribe (ST), or Other Backward Class (OBC). Required for government reservations, admissions, and schemes.
issuing_authority: Revenue Department – Sub-Divisional Magistrate (SDM) / Tehsildar / District Collector
required_documents: Proof of caste of parents or grandparents (school certificate, old caste certificate)
steps: Visit the Sub-Divisional Magistrate (SDM) office or apply online through state portal
Fill the prescribed application form
Attach all supporting documents and affidavit
Application verified by local patwari/revenue officer
Certificate issued within 15–30 days (7–10 days in most states via online portal)
fees: Free or nominal (₹10–₹50 depending on state)
portal: Apply at your state's service portal (e.g., edistrict.up.gov.in for UP)

name: Income Certificate
purpose: Certifies the total annual income of a family from all sources. Required for BPL card, government scheme eligibility, college admissions, and fee waivers.
issuing_authority: Revenue Department – Tehsildar / SDM / District Magistrate
steps: Visit tehsil office or apply online through state e-services portal
Fill income certificate application form
Attach documents and income declaration affidavit
Revenue inspector or patwari verifies income locally
Certificate issued by Tehsildar within 7–15 days
portal: Apply at your state's e-district portal

name: Voter ID (EPIC Card)
purpose: Electoral Photo Identity Card issued to all eligible Indian voters (18+). Used as identity proof and required to cast votes in elections.
issuing_authority: Election Commission of India – Electoral Registration Officer (ERO)
required_documents: Age proof: Birth certificate, Class 10 marksheet, Aadhaar
Address proof: Aadhaar, utility bill, bank passbook
steps: Go to voters.eci.gov.in or National Voter Services Portal (NVSP)
Click 'New Registration' and fill Form 6
Upload documents and photograph
Booth Level Officer (BLO) will verify at your address
Voter ID dispatched to your address within 30–60 days. Can also download e-EPIC

name: Birth Certificate
purpose: Official record of a child's birth. Required for school admissions, passports, government scheme eligibility, and legal identity proof.
issuing_authority: Municipal Corporation / Gram Panchayat / Registrar of Births and Deaths
required_documents: Hospital discharge certificate or birth proof from hospital
steps: Register birth at hospital within 21 days (hospitals usually do this automatically)
If not registered, visit Municipal Corporation/Panchayat with documents
Fill Form No. 1 under RBD Act
Pay nominal fee and receive birth certificate
For delayed registration (after 1 year), obtain Magistrate's order
fees: Free within 21 days; ₹2–₹50 after that (varies by municipality)

name: Domicile Certificate
purpose: Certifies that a person is a resident/domicile of a particular state. Required for state government jobs, state quota seats in colleges, and many state-specific schemes.
required_documents: Proof of residence for minimum required years (3–15 years depending on state)
steps: Visit tehsil office or state e-district portal
Fill the domicile certificate application form
Attach proof of continuous residence in the state
Revenue inspector verifies residence
Tehsildar issues certificate within 15–30 days
fees: Free or nominal (₹10–₹100 depending on state)
portal: Apply at your state's e-district portal

name: Driving Licence
purpose: Official licence to drive motor vehicles on Indian roads. Required for operating any motor vehicle and widely accepted as identity and address proof.
issuing_authority: Regional Transport Office (RTO) under Ministry of Road Transport and Highways
required_documents: Age proof: Aadhaar, Birth Certificate, or Passport
Address proof: Aadhaar, Utility Bill, or Voter ID
Learner's Licence (required before applying for permanent DL)
Medical certificate (Form 1A) for commercial vehicle licence
steps: Step 1: Apply for Learner's Licence online at parivahan.gov.in or at RTO
Step 2: Pass the Learner's Licence test (traffic rules and signs)
Step 3: After 30 days, apply for Permanent Driving Licence
Step 4: Appear for driving test at RTO
Step 5: DL issued within 7 days of passing test or dispatched by post
fees: ₹200–₹1,000 depending on vehicle class (two-wheeler, four-wheeler, etc.)
